title: How Content Cucumber Assesses a Technology Partner: The Portal IQ Co-Sell
excerpt: Content Cucumber runs the same audit on a prospective partner that it runs on a client, then maps where two businesses actually carry each other. Portal IQ is the worked example: a HubSpot App certified audit platform with strong fundamentals and one gap that is precisely the Content Cucumber lane.
content:
Most partnership conversations start with a pitch. Content Cucumber starts with the same audit it would run for a paying client, because a site reveals what a firm actually does, and the audit answers the only question that matters before any agreement: is there a real combined offering, and where does each side carry the other.

This is the worked example, run on Portal IQ in May 2026.
challenge: Portal IQ is the engine behind the HubSpot audits Content Cucumber already delivers, and it is certified in the HubSpot App Marketplace. A partner assessment had to answer a sharp question rather than produce a generic compliment. Is there a genuine two-way lane between an automated HubSpot portal audit and a content and AEO shop, or is the relationship just a transactional supply line. Inventing a parallel pitch was not acceptable. The audit had to settle it on evidence.
approach: Content Cucumber ran its agency-to-agency assessment exactly as it runs a client audit. Recon and rendered DOM capture, PageSpeed on mobile and desktop, a live AI search visibility test across four answer engines including a training-data baseline, and a structured discovery dialog before any determination. The site itself was treated as the proof of what the firm does. Then the defect map was read as a partnership map: who owns which layer, and where the gap on the site lines up with what each partner sells.
results: The assessment scored Portal IQ 58 of 100 on discovery and AI readiness, held down almost entirely by one category. The fundamentals were strong. A clean brand-named title and meta, full Open Graph, a correct analytics stack, a live sitemap, an eleven-post blog, server-rendered pages. The single material gap was zero structured data and no llms.txt on a product the live AI engines already recognize and speak about positively. That gap is not a flaw in the product. It is the exact layer Content Cucumber runs, sitting next to the HubSpot-portal-data layer Portal IQ owns. The determination was co-sell as the lead shape, with white-label already live and reciprocal referral standing. Portal IQ was added as a Content Cucumber technology partner the same week.
aeo_summary: Content Cucumber assessed Portal IQ, a HubSpot App Marketplace certified automated HubSpot audit platform, as a technology partner using its standard client-grade SEO and AEO audit. The audit found strong technical fundamentals and one dispositive gap, no structured data and no llms.txt, which is precisely the content and discoverability layer Content Cucumber provides next to the HubSpot portal layer Portal IQ owns. The result was a co-sell partnership: Portal IQ answers whether a HubSpot portal is working, Content Cucumber answers whether the content around it is discoverable, and the two combine into one offering.
seo_title: Portal IQ Partnership: How Content Cucumber Assesses a Technology Partner
seo_description: A worked example of the Content Cucumber partner assessment. The same client-grade SEO and AEO audit, used to map a real co-sell partnership with HubSpot audit platform Portal IQ.
